name: Coral Bento Editorial Product Magazine Landing
description: A warm off-white (#FBF8F4) bento grid layout with asymmetric panels of varying height — NOT a hero/features/pricing template but a magazine editorial about a product. Each bento panel has a corner-anchored monospace label in 10px uppercase tracking-widest (e.g., 'SIGNAL PROCESSING', 'AGENT STATUS', '< BEFORE / AFTER >') pinned to the top-left with 12px inset. Headlines in Instrument Serif at 48–72px paired with Inter body text and JetBrains Mono for all data, metrics, and code snippets. One large dark panel (#1A1A2E) contains an animated waveform or oscilloscope-style SVG visualization with coral-colored (#F97316) stroke paths. A side-by-side before/after comparison uses a light-surface card vs. a dark-surface card to show transformation. A horizontal-scrolling row of agent-status chips (pill shapes with a colored dot indicator + monospace label) overflows the grid edge. The grid gaps are 1px hairlines that become structural, not just spacing. This page sells by editorial confidence, not conversion psychology.

vibes.mood: Feels like a feature spread in a design magazine that happens to have working CTAs — the bento grid says 'we art-directed this like a physical layout,' the corner-anchored monospace labels say 'this is a system, not a template,' and the dark waveform panel says 'there's actual engineering under the editorial surface.' You don't scroll to find the pricing; you scroll because the layout keeps revealing new information like turning pages.

meta.audience: Design-literate product evaluators who distrust template-style landing pages — those who are more likely to sign up for a product that looks like it was art-directed than one that looks like it was A/B tested
